title: Short-circuit logic should be preferred in boolean contexts
description: Short-circuit evaluation is an evaluation strategy for Boolean operators, that doesnâ€™t evaluates the second argument of the operator if it is not needed to determine the result of the operation. C# provides logical operators that implement short-circuit evaluation: && and ||, as well as non-short-circuit versions: & and |. Unlike short-circuit operators, non-short-circuit ones evaluate both operands and afterwards perform the logical operation.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: Use short-circuit operators (&&, ||) instead of non-short-circuit operators (&, |) in boolean contexts.

logic.reasoning: Non-short-circuit operators evaluate both operands which can cause side effects or null reference exceptions. Short-circuit operators are safer.
